name: Screen Shake
description: A short camera offset, rotation, or impulse profile used to emphasize impact while preserving play readability.
design_notes: Screen shake is a feedback layer and should never be the only way to communicate damage, danger, or success. Treat shake as authored profiles with caps, priority, per-axis scaling, and reduced-motion controls. Strong profiles should be rare enough to keep their meaning.
design_purpose: Sell weight for impacts, explosions, landings, and heavy world events.
Differentiate impact categories through authored camera response profiles.
Reinforce feedback without changing simulation, hit detection, or player control.
player_fantasy: The player feels impact and scale through the camera while still retaining control and orientation.

parameters.name: trauma_add_per_event
parameters.description: Contribution added to the current shake accumulator.

parameters.name: trauma_decay_rate
parameters.description: Rate at which accumulated shake falls back to zero.

parameters.name: max_positional_offset_pixels
parameters.description: Maximum screen-space offset applied at full intensity.

parameters.name: max_rotational_offset_degrees
parameters.description: Maximum roll, yaw, or pitch perturbation.

parameters.name: reduced_motion_intensity_scale
parameters.description: Player setting multiplier applied to shake offsets and rotations.

parameters.name: shake_seed_mode
parameters.description: Noise seed policy used for replay or multiplayer consistency.

edge_cases: Repeated rapid events should accumulate through a cap rather than stacking into unreadable camera motion.
Reticles, health bars, subtitles, and interaction prompts should not inherit shake unless the UI is intentionally diegetic.
Pausing during a shake should define whether decay freezes, completes instantly, or resumes from remaining trauma.
Cinematic cameras, split-screen, spectating, and photo mode need opt-in shake routing.
common_bugs: Shake is applied before camera collision and pushes the camera through walls.
Multiple systems write offsets independently and fight or double the final displacement.
Reduced-motion settings scale visual shake but not vibration, audio rumble, or FOV pulses.
Deterministic replays diverge because shake consumes the same random stream as gameplay.
balancing_notes: Use small shake profiles frequently and reserve large profiles for events that deserve momentary attention.
Camera motion should never be required to understand damage direction, hit confirmation, or timing.
accessibility_notes: Expose intensity and disable options, and pair impacts with non-motion cues such as hit flash, sound, haptics, or UI markers.
Avoid high-frequency or sustained shake patterns that can cause discomfort or obscure precision tasks.

implementation_notes.unity: Apply shake as a late camera offset after target follow and before final composition; with Cinemachine, use impulse profiles scaled by reduced-motion settings.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep a shake accumulator on the Camera2D or Camera3D rig, sample noise after the target position is resolved, and keep UI CanvasLayer elements stable unless opted in.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use Camera Shake Sources, Camera Modifiers, or PlayerCameraManager profiles with per-player intensity scaling and separate haptic routing.
implementation_notes.web: Apply shake to the render camera or canvas view matrix after simulation, keep UI layers separate, and use deterministic seeds when replay consistency matters.

example_games.title: Nuclear Throne
example_games.note: Uses frequent shake to emphasize weapon impact.

example_games.title: Celeste
example_games.note: Uses restrained shake to support impacts and transitions.
